rationale: tenía → imperfecto: describes an ongoing physical state (hunger) that existed as background context.
fui → pretérito: going to the market was a single, completed trip on that specific Saturday.

parts: llenos de frutas y la gente
rationale: estaban → imperfecto: describes the scene as I found it — a background, descriptive state.
caminaba → imperfecto: people walking is ongoing background activity, setting the atmosphere.
llamó → pretérito: de repente signals a sudden, punctual interruption — a completed action.
ofreció → pretérito: offering the sample was a single, discrete completed event.
era → imperfecto: describes an inherent quality of the mango — a descriptive state, not a change.
compré → pretérito: buying two kilos was a specific, completed transaction.

parts: que el mercado siempre me alegra el día.
rationale: volvía → imperfecto: walking home was an ongoing action that served as the background frame.
pensé → pretérito: the thought occurred as a single, completed mental event within that walk.
